behaviors.description: Valid JSON payloads containing markdown-heavy strings parse successfully and proceed through normal hook input handling.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, because the behavior is about parser output being accepted by downstream hook-input handling (module handoff), not only a single pure parse helper. PASS2-AUDIT: Unit is tempting, but the text "proceed through normal hook input handling" requires wiring parser + handler path. System rejected by behavior text "local"/no requirement for real process or OS I/O here; an in-process harness can reproduce the failure boundary. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none.
behaviors.test_description: Run an in-process test that feeds valid JSON containing code fences, backticks, and `$vars` through the same parser+hook-input handling pipeline used in production, with a fake in-memory trace sink. Assert parse success, handler continuation, and absence of parse-failure trace.

behaviors.description: Truly malformed JSON payloads produce a `json_parse_failed` trace record with enough context to diagnose the malformed section.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, since this validates parser error -> trace-record construction with diagnostic context across components. PASS2-AUDIT: Unit could miss real handoff bugs between parse error object and trace formatter/sink contract. System rejected by behavior text: it asks for trace record semantics, not real process/stdin behavior. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none.
behaviors.test_description: Use an in-process malformed JSON fixture and execute parser plus trace-emission path with an in-memory sink. Assert a `json_parse_failed` record is emitted and includes expected context fields (e.g., offset/snippet/error message) sufficient to locate malformed section.

behaviors.description: The local error-policy path maps parse failure to a handled result (no throw) and marks telemetry intent.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, because it checks orchestration between parse failure signal, error-policy mapper, and telemetry-intent marker. PASS2-AUDIT: Unit may cover mapper logic alone, but real failure boundary is contract wiring among modules. System rejected by concrete text "local error-policy path" and no OS/network/subprocess need. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none.
behaviors.test_description: Inject a parse-failure object through the local policy entrypoint that is wired as in production. Assert returned handled result (no exception) and telemetry intent flag/state are set for downstream reporting.

behaviors.description: In real process execution, malformed stdin does not crash the hook process and still emits the expected trace signal before exit.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: System, explicitly required by "real process execution" and malformed stdin behavior. PASS2-AUDIT: Integration is insufficient because mocks/fakes can hide stdin buffering, process lifecycle, and exit-order issues. Rejection-gate: Integration disqualified by behavior text "real process execution" and "malformed stdin". LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none.
behaviors.test_description: Spawn the actual hook executable as a subprocess, pipe malformed bytes on stdin, wait for exit, and assert: process does not crash unexpectedly, expected trace signal is emitted before termination, and exit behavior matches policy.

behaviors.description: A real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink) produces the expected trace artifact when malformed input is supplied.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: System, because the behavior names a real end-to-end invocation path including stdin and artifact emission side effects. PASS2-AUDIT: Integration would miss process I/O and real sink emission details (ordering/flushing/path semantics). Rejection-gate: Integration disqualified by concrete phrase "real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink)". LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real hook entrypoint in subprocess mode with malformed stdin and a real configured trace sink target (e.g., file/stdout collector). Assert the expected malformed-input trace artifact is present with correct type/content.

behaviors.description: Regression tests distinguish malformed-JSON failures from valid-markdown payloads, preventing false attribution to markdown content itself.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, because this is classification behavior across parser + failure-labeling/trace path for two input classes. PASS2-AUDIT: Unit could test parser alone, but false attribution often appears at module handoff where failures are labeled/reported. System rejected by behavior text: no requirement for real process/OS, only regression distinction logic. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none.
behaviors.test_description: Create paired fixtures (valid markdown-heavy JSON vs malformed JSON) and run both through the in-process production wiring for parse + error classification + trace labeling. Assert only malformed input yields `json_parse_failed` and valid markdown payload follows non-error path.
